name: ScyllaDB
description: High-performance distributed wide-column NoSQL database, a C++ rewrite of Apache Cassandra using the Seastar framework

license.comment: As of December 2024, moved from AGPL-3.0 to source available license. ScyllaDB OSS AGPL 6.2 is the final open source release. Enterprise 2025.1+ is source available.

hosting-options.comment: Self-hosted via Docker/packages, ScyllaDB Cloud as official managed service. Also available via AWS Marketplace.

initial-release.comment: Released as open source in September 2015.

primary-language.comment: Written in C++20 using the Seastar asynchronous framework, replacing Cassandra's Java implementation.

data-model.comment: Wide-column store compatible with Cassandra CQL. Also provides DynamoDB-compatible key-value API (Alternator).

topology.comment: Peer-to-peer architecture like Cassandra. All nodes are equal with no single point of failure.

sharding.comment: Uses consistent hashing for data distribution. Additionally implements shard-per-core architecture for internal parallelism.

replication-factor.comment: Replication factor of 3 recommended for production. Configurable per keyspace using replication strategies.

multi-region.comment: Native support for multi-datacenter deployments. ScyllaDB Cloud supports multi-region clusters.

cap-position.comment: Defaults to AP like Cassandra. Tunable consistency per-operation from ONE to ALL. LWT provides linearizability.

consistency-model.comment: Tunable consistency from eventual (ONE) to strong (QUORUM) to linearizable (via LWT with Paxos).

acid-transactions.comment: No full ACID transactions. Lightweight transactions (LWT) provide linearizable compare-and-set operations for single partitions only.

transaction-scope.comment: LWT limited to single-partition operations. Conditional batches support multiple statements but all must target same partition key.

isolation-levels.comment: Traditional isolation levels don't apply. Uses tunable consistency levels (ONE, QUORUM, ALL, SERIAL, etc.) instead.

distributed-transactions.comment: No cross-partition distributed transactions. LWT uses Paxos but limited to single partition.

query-language.comment: Full CQL compatibility with Cassandra. Also supports DynamoDB API via Alternator.

sql-compatibility.comment: CQL is SQL-like but not SQL-compatible. No JOINs, subqueries, or cross-partition aggregations.

secondary-indexes.comment: Global and local secondary indexes supported. Also supports materialized views.

joins.comment: No JOIN support. Data model should be denormalized for query patterns.

aggregations.comment: Basic aggregations (COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX) supported within a single partition. User-defined aggregates available.

full-text-search.comment: No built-in full-text search. External solutions like Elasticsearch often used.

max-cluster-size.comment: Discord migrated from 177 Cassandra nodes to 72 ScyllaDB nodes storing trillions of messages. Comcast migrated from 962 to 78 nodes.

write-throughput.comment: ScyllaDB Enterprise 2024.1 achieves 50% higher throughput than previous release. Discord handles trillions of messages.

read-latency-p99.comment: Discord achieved 15ms p99 read latency vs 40-125ms with Cassandra. ScyllaDB typically achieves sub-10ms p99.

auto-scaling.comment: Nodes can be added without downtime. ScyllaDB Cloud supports managed scaling but not fully automatic.

hot-spot-handling.comment: Hot spots addressed through data model design. Shard-per-core architecture helps distribute load within nodes.

zero-downtime-upgrades.comment: Rolling upgrades supported. ScyllaDB Operator automates rolling upgrades in Kubernetes.

online-schema-changes.comment: Schema changes propagate via gossip protocol without downtime.

point-in-time-recovery.comment: Available in ScyllaDB Cloud and Enterprise. Combines snapshots with CDC for recovery.

backup-strategy.comment: Snapshot and incremental backups to S3 or GCS. ScyllaDB Manager provides backup automation. CDC enables streaming backups.

kubernetes-operator.comment: Official ScyllaDB Operator for Kubernetes. Automates deployment, scaling, backups, upgrades, and self-healing.

rust-driver.comment: Official async Rust driver using Tokio. Production-ready since version 1.0. Shard-aware for optimal performance.

go-driver.comment: Official shard-aware Go driver (gocqlx fork). Also compatible with standard GoCQL driver.

java-driver.comment: Official shard-aware Java driver. Also compatible with DataStax/Apache Cassandra Java driver.

dotnet-driver.comment: Compatible with DataStax C# driver for Cassandra. No ScyllaDB-specific driver.

python-driver.comment: Official Python driver (fork of cassandra-driver) with shard-awareness.

orm-support.comment: Limited ORM support due to wide-column data model. Spring Data Cassandra compatible.

open-source.comment: As of December 2024, source available license (not OSI-approved). AGPL 6.2 is final open source version.

free-tier.comment: Source available license allows free production use up to 50 vCPU and 10 TB per organization.

pricing-model.comment: ScyllaDB Cloud pricing based on node type, count, and deployment region. On-demand hourly or annual subscription.

cost-estimate-small.comment: Varies by instance type and region. Contact sales for specific pricing. Claims 1/5th cost of DynamoDB for equivalent workloads.

github-stars.comment: As of December 2024. Repository has 15.2k stars and 1.5k forks.

stackoverflow-tags.comment: Estimated based on ScyllaDB tag. Community primarily uses ScyllaDB Forum and Slack.

change-data-capture.comment: Built-in CDC stores changes in CQL tables. Integrates with Kafka via Debezium connector. Shard-aware CDC libraries for Rust, Go, Java.

watch-subscribe.comment: No native push-based change notifications. Feature request for RethinkDB-style change feeds opened in 2015 remains unimplemented. CDC polling or Kafka/Debezium required for change streaming.

triggers.comment: No trigger support. GitHub issue #2205 open since 2017 but deprioritized due to lack of demand. Unlike Cassandra, would not use Java-based implementation.

event-streaming.comment: Official shard-aware Kafka CDC Source Connector (Debezium-based). Pulsar works via Cassandra-compatible connector but not shard-aware. No native Kinesis integration.
